260	  \]
261	  where, on the \((i,j)\)-component, leg \(a\) pushes the \(i\)-th factor forward along the
262	  overlap immersion \(f_{ij}\) using the unit of the pullback--pushforward adjunction
263	  together with the pushforward-composition comparison
264	  \((\iota_i)_{*} \cong (\iota_i \circ f_{ij})_{*}\circ f_{ij}^{*}\) (the dual of
265	  \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}), and leg \(b\) pushes the \(j\)-th factor forward and
266	  additionally transports it across the inverse transition isomorphism \((g_{ij})^{-1}\)
267	  and the glue datum's compatibility identity, so that both legs land on the common overlap
268	  sheaf \((\iota_i \circ f_{ij})_{*}(f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i)\). The glued sheaf is the
269	  equalizer
270	  \[
271	    \mathcal{M} \;=\; \operatorname{eq}(a,b) \;\hookrightarrow\; \prod_{i} (\iota_i)_{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i,
272	  \]
273	  the sub-sheaf of tuples of pushed-forward chart sections whose two overlap restrictions
274	  agree -- precisely the compatible families, now realised as a categorical limit rather than
275	  a bespoke presheaf. The cocycle conditions (C1)/(C2) on the family \((g_{ij})\) are
276	  \emph{not} consumed in forming this equalizer object: the limit exists for any family of
277	  transition maps. They are instead what make the equalizer descend correctly, and are
278	  exactly the hypotheses pinned down downstream when the restriction isomorphisms
279	  \cref{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso} are produced. This specialises effective descent
280	  of sheaves of modules to the concrete open cover \(\{\iota_i : U_i \hookrightarrow X\}\)
281	  supplied by a scheme glue datum, and is the form in which the Grassmannian's
282	  tautological bundle and quotient are built.
283	
284	  The glued sheaf comes with three further structures, each recorded as its own block
285	  downstream: the \emph{restriction isomorphisms} \(\rho_i : \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}
286	  \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{M}_i\) compatible with the \(g_{ij}\) over the overlaps
287	  \cref{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso}; the \emph{characterisation up to unique
288	  isomorphism} by the restriction property \cref{lem:gr_modules_glue_unique}; and the
289	  \emph{descent of morphisms} -- a family \(\phi_i : \mathcal{M}_i \to \mathcal{N}_i\)
290	  commuting with the transition isomorphisms glues to a unique \(\phi : \mathcal{M} \to
291	  \mathcal{N}\) with \(\rho_i^{\mathcal{N}} \circ \iota_i^{*}\phi = \phi_i \circ
292	  \rho_i^{\mathcal{M}}\) \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom}.
293	\end{definition}
294	
295	\begin{definition}[Restriction isomorphisms of the glued sheaf]
296	  \label{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso}
297	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueRestrictionIso}
298	  % NOTE: forward declaration (planned work); the \lean{} target is not yet realised.
299	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso,
300	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges}
301	  Let \(\mathcal{M}\) be the glued sheaf of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}. For each
302	  chart index \(i\) there is a \emph{restriction isomorphism} of sheaves of
303	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\)-modules
304	  \[
305	    \rho_i \;:\; \iota_i^{*}\,\mathcal{M} \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \mathcal{M}_i,
306	  \]
307	  compatible with the transition isomorphisms: over each overlap \(V_{ij}\) the two
308	  restrictions \(\rho_i\) and \(\rho_j\), transported to the common overlap, differ
309	  exactly by \(g_{ij}\).
310	\end{definition}
311	\begin{proof}
312	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso,
313	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges}
314	  By the equalizer-of-pushforwards description \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}, a section of
315	  \(\mathcal{M}\) over an open of the form \(\iota_i^{-1}V\), restricted to the chart
316	  \(U_i\), is recovered from and recovers its \(i\)-th component
317	  \(s_i \in \Gamma(\mathcal{M}_i, \cdot)\) -- the \(i\)-th factor of the product
318	  \(\prod_i (\iota_i)_{*}\mathcal{M}_i\) into which the equalizer embeds; the
319	  chart-restriction bridge \cref{lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso} identifies the pullback
320	  \(\iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}\) with this restriction, and the \(i\)-th projection
321	  \((s_\bullet) \mapsto s_i\) is the isomorphism \(\rho_i\) (with inverse extending a chart
322	  section by the overlap relation).
323	  The overlap compatibility is the defining relation of the compatible families: over
324	  \(V_{ij}\) the components \(s_i, s_j\) are related by \(g_{ij}\), which is precisely the
325	  statement that \(\rho_i\) and \(\rho_j\) differ by \(g_{ij}\) after transport to
326	  \(V_{ij}\); the triple-overlap coherence needed for this to be consistent is (C2),
327	  whose transports are aligned by \cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport} and
328	  \cref{lem:gr_glueData_bridges}.
329	\end{proof}
330	
331	\begin{lemma}[Uniqueness of the glued sheaf up to canonical isomorphism]
332	  \label{lem:gr_modules_glue_unique}
333	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glue_unique}
334	  % NOTE: forward declaration (planned work); the \lean{} target is not yet realised.
335	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue, def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso,
336	    lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
337	  Suppose \(\mathcal{M}'\) is another sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules equipped with
338	  isomorphisms \(\rho'_i : \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}' \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{M}_i\)
339	  compatible with the \(g_{ij}\) over the overlaps. Then there is a unique isomorphism
340	  \(\mathcal{M} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{M}'\) intertwining the restriction
341	  isomorphisms, i.e.\ with \(\rho'_i \circ \iota_i^{*}(\cdot) = \rho_i\) for every \(i\).
342	\end{lemma}
343	\begin{proof}
344	  \uses{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso, lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
345	  A morphism of sheaves of modules on \(X\) is determined by, and may be assembled from,
346	  its restrictions to the members of the open cover \(\{\iota_i\}\), agreeing on the
347	  overlaps -- the locality and gluing of sections
348	  (\cref{lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}) applied to the internal hom. The
349	  intertwining condition forces the restriction over \(U_i\) to be
350	  \((\rho'_i)^{-1} \circ \rho_i : \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M} \to \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}'\);
351	  these local isomorphisms agree on each overlap \(V_{ij}\) because both
352	  \((\rho_i)\) and \((\rho'_i)\) are compatible with the \emph{same} transition family
353	  \((g_{ij})\) (\cref{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso}). By locality they glue to a
354	  unique global isomorphism \(\mathcal{M} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{M}'\) with the
355	  required property; uniqueness is the separation half of the same gluing.
356	\end{proof}
357	
358	\begin{definition}[Descent of morphisms along the cover]
359	  \label{def:gr_modules_glueHom}
360	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueHom}
361	  % NOTE: forward declaration (planned work); the \lean{} target is not yet realised.
362	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue, def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso,
363	    lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
364	  Let \((\mathcal{M}_i, g_{ij})\) and \((\mathcal{N}_i, h_{ij})\) be two glued data
365	  over the same glue datum \(D\), with glued sheaves \(\mathcal{M}, \mathcal{N}\) and
366	  restriction isomorphisms \(\rho^{\mathcal{M}}_i, \rho^{\mathcal{N}}_i\). Given a family
367	  of \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\)-linear morphisms \(\phi_i : \mathcal{M}_i \to \mathcal{N}_i\)
368	  commuting with the transition isomorphisms on every overlap (i.e.\ \(h_{ij}\) intertwines
369	  the pullbacks of \(\phi_i\) and \(\phi_j\) over \(V_{ij}\)), there is a unique morphism
370	  of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\phi : \mathcal{M} \to \mathcal{N}\) with
371	  \[
372	    \rho^{\mathcal{N}}_i \circ \iota_i^{*}\phi \;=\; \phi_i \circ \rho^{\mathcal{M}}_i
373	    \qquad \text{for every } i.
374	  \]
375	\end{definition}
376	\begin{proof}
377	  \uses{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso, lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
378	  On each chart the prescribed identity defines a local morphism
379	  \((\rho^{\mathcal{N}}_i)^{-1} \circ \phi_i \circ \rho^{\mathcal{M}}_i :
380	  \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M} \to \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{N}\). The commutation of the \(\phi_i\)
381	  with the transition isomorphisms \((g_{ij}, h_{ij})\), together with the overlap
382	  compatibility of the \(\rho\)-families (\cref{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso}), makes
383	  these local morphisms agree on every overlap \(V_{ij}\). By the locality and gluing of
384	  sections (\cref{lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}) applied to the internal hom they
385	  promote to a unique global morphism \(\phi : \mathcal{M} \to \mathcal{N}\) restricting
386	  to each \(\phi_i\) through the \(\rho_i\) -- the descent of \(\mathrm{Hom}\) along the
387	  cover.
388	\end{proof}
389	
390	\subsection{Pullback of free sheaves along an arbitrary morphism}
391	\label{sec:grquot_pullback_free}
392	
393	The functor of points (\cref{def:grassmannian_functor}) acts on morphisms by pullback, so
394	it needs the pullback of the trivial bundle to be again trivial --
395	\(\varphi^{*}\mathcal{O}_T^{\,\oplus I} \cong \mathcal{O}_{T'}^{\,\oplus I}\) -- for an
396	\emph{arbitrary} scheme morphism \(\varphi\). Mathlib's pullback-of-free comparison applies
397	only when the underlying site functor \(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,\varphi\) is \emph{final}; the
398	following three blocks remove that restriction for every morphism, and the resulting
399	pullback machinery preserves rank-\(d\) local freeness.

390	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
391	
392	/-! ### Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism to a triple overlap
393	
394	To state the triple-overlap multiplicativity (C2) of a module descent datum we must
395	transport each transition isomorphism `g_ij`, living on the overlap `V_ij`, to the common
396	triple overlap `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik`. The transport pulls `g_ij` back along a
397	projection `p : V_ijk ⟶ V_ij` and reassociates the iterated pullbacks via the pseudofunctor
398	comparison `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`. The three scheme-level `glueData_bridge_*`
399	identities below (consequences of `t_fac`, `pullback.condition` and `cocycle`) line up the
400	endpoints of the three transports so that the cocycle equation is well typed. -/
401	
402	/-- **Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism along a morphism**
403	(`lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport`). Given a transition isomorphism
404	`g : a^*Mᵢ ≅ b^*Mⱼ` over `V` and a morphism `p : W ⟶ V`, transport it to `W` as an
405	isomorphism `(p ≫ a)^*Mᵢ ≅ (p ≫ b)^*Mⱼ`, by pulling `g` back along `p` and reassociating
406	the iterated pullbacks through `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`.
407	
408	Project-local: this is the pullback-pseudofunctor packaging that lets the three transition
409	isomorphisms attached to a triple of charts be compared on a single triple overlap; Mathlib
410	has no descent-of-modules-over-a-scheme-glue-datum API. -/
411	noncomputable def pullbackBaseChangeTransport {W V : Scheme.{u}} (p : W ⟶ V)
412	    {Yi Yj : Scheme.{u}} (a : V ⟶ Yi) (b : V ⟶ Yj)
413	    {Mi : Yi.Modules} {Mj : Yj.Modules}
414	    (g : (Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj Mj) :
415	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ a)).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ b)).obj Mj :=
416	  (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p a).symm.app Mi ≪≫
417	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).mapIso g ≪≫
418	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app Mj
419	
420	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (source): on `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the two projections to
421	`V_ij` and `V_ik` followed by the overlap immersions `f_ij`, `f_ik` agree as morphisms to
422	`U_i`. This is the pullback condition; it identifies the sources of the `ij`- and
423	`ik`-transports. Project-local helper for the module cocycle (C2). -/
424	theorem glueData_bridge_src (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
425	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i j
426	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i k := pullback.condition
427	
428	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (middle): the `ij`-transition's target leg
429	`p^{ij} ≫ (t_ij ≫ f_ji)` to `U_j` coincides with the `jk`-transition's source leg
430	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ f_jk`. Follows from `t_fac` and the pullback condition; it identifies
431	the target of the `ij`-transport with the source of the `jk`-transport. Project-local helper
432	for the module cocycle (C2). -/
433	theorem glueData_bridge_mid (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
434	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)
435	      = (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ D.f j k := by
436	  rw [Category.assoc, pullback.condition, ← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc, D.t_fac i j k,
437	    Category.assoc]
438	
439	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (target): the `jk`-transition's target leg
440	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ (t_jk ≫ f_kj)` to `U_k` coincides with the `ik`-transition's target
441	leg `p^{ik} ≫ (t_ik ≫ f_ki)`. This is the heart of the cocycle, derived from `t_fac`, the
442	pullback condition, `t_inv` and `cocycle`; it identifies the target of the composite
443	`jk`-after-`ij` transport with the target of the `ik`-transport. Project-local helper for
444	the module cocycle (C2). -/
445	theorem glueData_bridge_tgt (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
446	    (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j)
447	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) := by
448	  have key : pullback.fst (D.f k i) (D.f k j) ≫ D.f k i
449	      = D.t' k i j ≫ pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.t i k ≫ D.f k i := by
450	    rw [D.t_fac_assoc k i j, ← Category.assoc (D.t k i) (D.t i k), D.t_inv, Category.id_comp]
451	  rw [Category.assoc, ← D.t_fac_assoc j k i,
452	    ← @pullback.condition _ _ _ _ _ (D.f k i) (D.f k j) _, key, D.cocycle_assoc i j k]
453	
454	/-- **Gluing a sheaf of modules along an open cover given by a scheme glue datum**
455	(`def:scheme_modules_glue`). From a glue datum `D`, per-chart sheaves of modules `M i`,
456	and transition isomorphisms `g i j` comparing the two charts' sheaves over the overlap
457	`V (i,j)` (after pullback), produces a glued sheaf of `O_{D.glued}`-modules.
458	
459	Project-local: Mathlib has no module descent over a scheme glue datum. -/
460	noncomputable def glue (D : Scheme.GlueData)
461	    (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
462	    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
463	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
464	    -- (C1) self-identity: over the diagonal overlap `V (i,i)` (where `t i i = 𝟙`) the
465	    -- transition isomorphism is the identity, i.e. the canonical isomorphism induced by
466	    -- `f i i = t i i ≫ f i i` (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue` (C1)).
467	    (_hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
468	        (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
469	    -- (C2) triple-overlap multiplicativity: over each triple overlap
470	    -- `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the base-change transports
471	    -- (`pullbackBaseChangeTransport`) of the three transition isomorphisms `g_ij`, `g_jk`,
472	    -- `g_ik` satisfy `ĝ_jk ∘ ĝ_ij = ĝ_ik`. The three `glueData_bridge_*` identities, applied
473	    -- through `pullbackCongr`, line up the endpoints so the equation is well typed
474	    -- (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue` (C2), `lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport`).
475	    (_hC2 : ∀ i j k,
476	        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
477	            (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (g i j) ≪≫
478	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_mid D i j k)).app (M j) ≪≫
479	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i))
480	            (D.f j k) (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j) (g j k) ≪≫
481	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_tgt D i j k)).app (M k)
482	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_src D i j k)).app (M i) ≪≫
483	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
484	            (D.f i k) (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) (g i k)) :
485	    D.glued.Modules :=
486	  -- **Effective descent as an equalizer of pushforwards.** The glued sheaf is the
487	  -- equalizer of the two canonical maps `∏ᵢ (ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ ⇉ ∏_{ij} (j_ij)_* (f_ij^* Mᵢ)`
488	  -- (`j_ij = f_ij ≫ ιᵢ : V_ij ↪ X`): the first map restricts the `i`-th chart section to
489	  -- `V_ij`, the second restricts the `j`-th and transports it across the transition `g_ij`,
490	  -- using the glue condition `(t_ij ≫ f_ji) ≫ ιⱼ = f_ij ≫ ιᵢ`. The cocycle hypotheses
491	  -- `_hC1`/`_hC2` are not needed to *construct* the object (they pin down the chart
492	  -- restriction isomorphisms `glueRestrictionIso`, built downstream). Pushforward preserves
493	  -- the sheaf condition and limits, so this equalizer of sheaves of modules is again a sheaf.
494	  let Qfun : D.J × D.J → D.glued.Modules := fun p =>
495	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1)).obj
496	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
497	  let P : D.glued.Modules := ∏ᶜ fun i => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i)
498	  -- first leg: restrict the `p.1`-chart section to the overlap `V (p.1, p.2)`
499	  let aComp : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
500	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).obj (M p.1) ⟶ Qfun p := fun p =>
501	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
502	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app (M p.1)) ≫
503	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p.1 p.2) (D.ι p.1)).hom.app
504	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
505	  -- second leg: restrict the `p.2`-chart section, transport it across `g`, and reindex
506	  -- the immersion via the glue condition
507	  let bComp : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
508	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).obj (M p.2) ⟶ Qfun p := fun p =>
509	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
510	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
511	          (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫
512	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) (D.ι p.2)).hom.app
513	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).obj (M p.2)) ≫
514	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
515	        ((D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2)).map (g p.1 p.2).inv ≫
516	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
517	        (show (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2 = D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1 by
518	          rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p.1 p.2)).hom.app
519	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
520	  let a : P ⟶ ∏ᶜ Qfun := Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.1 ≫ aComp p
521	  let b : P ⟶ ∏ᶜ Qfun := Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.2 ≫ bComp p
522	  equalizer a b
523	
524	/-- **Lift into the glued sheaf** (`def:gr_modules_glueHom`-adjacent primitive): a family of
525	morphisms `k i : W ⟶ (ι_i)_* M_i` whose two overlap restrictions agree (the hypothesis
526	`hk`, stated against the two legs of the descent equalizer) lifts to a morphism
527	`W ⟶ glue D M g _ _`. This is `equalizer.lift` for the descent equalizer of pushforwards;
528	it is the vehicle by which the tautological quotient is assembled from the chart
529	quotients. Project-local. -/
530	noncomputable def glueLift (D : Scheme.GlueData)
531	    (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
532	    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
533	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
534	    (_hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
535	        (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
536	    (_hC2 : ∀ i j k,
537	        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
538	            (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (g i j) ≪≫
539	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_mid D i j k)).app (M j) ≪≫
540	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i))
541	            (D.f j k) (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j) (g j k) ≪≫
542	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_tgt D i j k)).app (M k)
543	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_src D i j k)).app (M i) ≪≫
544	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
545	            (D.f i k) (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) (g i k))
546	    {W : D.glued.Modules}
547	    (k : ∀ i, W ⟶ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i))
548	    (hk : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
549	      k p.1 ≫
550	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
551	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app (M p.1)) ≫
552	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p.1 p.2) (D.ι p.1)).hom.app
553	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))
554	        = k p.2 ≫
555	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
556	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
557	              (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫
558	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) (D.ι p.2)).hom.app
559	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).obj (M p.2)) ≫
560	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
561	            ((D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2)).map (g p.1 p.2).inv ≫
562	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
563	            (show (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2 = D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1 by
564	              rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p.1 p.2)).hom.app
565	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))) :
566	    W ⟶ glue D M g _hC1 _hC2 :=
567	  equalizer.lift (Pi.lift k) (by
568	    apply Pi.hom_ext
569	    intro p
570	    simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Pi.lift_π, Limits.Pi.lift_π_assoc]
571	    exact hk p)
572	
573	/-! ### Pullback of free sheaves along an arbitrary scheme morphism
574	
575	The functor of points (`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`) acts on morphisms by
576	pullback, and for that one needs `f^* (O^n) ≅ O^n` for an *arbitrary* scheme morphism
577	`f`. Mathlib's `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` supplies this only when the underlying
578	site functor `Opens.map f.base` is `Final`; the previous chapters discharged that `Final`
579	hypothesis only for open immersions and isomorphisms. The first lemma below removes the
580	restriction entirely: `Opens.map f.base` is `Final` for *every* scheme morphism, because
581	the structured-arrow category over any open `V` has a terminal object (the whole space
582	`⊤`). With that in hand, `pullbackFreeIso` and `pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` hold for all
583	morphisms. -/
584	
585	/-- For an arbitrary scheme morphism `φ`, the site functor `Opens.map φ.base` is `Final`:
586	over any open `V` of the target the structured-arrow category `{U : V ≤ φ⁻¹ U}` has the
587	terminal object `U = ⊤`, hence is connected. This is the missing ingredient that makes
588	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit`/`pullbackObjFreeIso` applicable to *every* morphism,
589	not just to open immersions. Project-local. -/
590	lemma opensMap_final {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) :
591	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base).Final := by
592	  constructor
593	  intro V
594	  set top : StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base) :=
595	    StructuredArrow.mk (Y := (⊤ : T.Opens)) (homOfLE le_top)
596	  haveI : Nonempty (StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base)) := ⟨top⟩
597	  apply zigzag_isConnected
598	  intro s t
599	  have hs : s ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
600	  have ht : t ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
601	  exact Relation.ReflTransGen.trans
602	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inl ⟨hs⟩))
603	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inr ⟨ht⟩))
604	
605	/-- **Pullback of a free sheaf of modules is free, for any scheme morphism**: for
606	`φ : T' ⟶ T` and an index type `I`, `φ^*(O_T^{⊕I}) ≅ O_{T'}^{⊕I}`. Built from
607	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` once `opensMap_final` supplies the `Final` instance.
608	Project-local. -/
609	noncomputable def pullbackFreeIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) (I : Type u) :
610	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)
611	      ≅ SheafOfModules.free (R := T'.ringCatSheaf) I := by
612	  haveI := opensMap_final φ
613	  exact SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso φ.toRingCatSheafHom I
614	
615	/-- The free-pullback comparison is natural in the base morphism: equal morphisms give
616	`pullbackFreeIso`s related by the `eqToHom` transport of their (differing) sources.
617	Project-local — used for the bundle-transition self-identity. -/
618	lemma pullbackFreeIso_eqToHom {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ) (I : Type u) :
619	    eqToHom (congrArg
620	        (fun α => (Scheme.Modules.pullback α).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)) h)
621	        ≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).hom
622	      = (pullbackFreeIso φ I).hom := by
623	  subst h; simp
624	
625	/-- Iso-level free-pullback cancellation: for equal base morphisms `φ = ψ`, the composite
626	`pullbackFreeIso φ ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ).symm` is the `eqToIso` transport between the
627	(differing) pullback sources. Proved generically (`φ`, `ψ` variables, `subst`), so applying
628	it never forces the kernel to whnf a concrete immersion — the leaner replacement for the
629	`.hom`-level cast chain in `bundleTransition_self`. Project-local. -/
630	lemma pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ)
631	    (I : Type u) :
632	    pullbackFreeIso φ I ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).symm
633	      = eqToIso (congrArg
634	          (fun α => (Scheme.Modules.pullback α).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I))
635	          h) := by
636	  subst h; simp
637	
638	/-- **Pullback preserves rank-`d` local freeness.** If `M` is locally free of rank `d` on
639	`T`, then `φ^* M` is locally free of rank `d` on `T'`, for any scheme morphism `φ`. The
640	chart cover `{U i}` of `T` trivialising `M` pulls back to the cover `{φ⁻¹ U i}` of `T'`;
641	on each member the restriction of `φ^* M` is identified with the pulled-back chart-free
642	sheaf via the pseudofunctor comparison `pullbackComp`, the factorisation
643	`φ ∘ (φ⁻¹ U i).ι = (φ ∣_ U i) ≫ (U i).ι` (`morphismRestrict_ι`), and `pullbackFreeIso`.
644	Project-local. -/
645	lemma pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) {M : T.Modules}
646	    {d : ℕ} (h : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank M d) :
647	    SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj M) d := by
648	  obtain ⟨ι, U, hcover, hloc⟩ := h
649	  refine ⟨ι, fun i => φ ⁻¹ᵁ (U i), Scheme.Hom.iSup_preimage_eq_top φ hcover, ?_⟩
650	  intro i
651	  obtain ⟨e⟩ := hloc i
652	  exact ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (φ ⁻¹ᵁ (U i)).ι φ).app M ≪≫
653	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (morphismRestrict_ι φ (U i)).symm).app M ≪≫
654	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (φ ∣_ (U i)) (U i).ι).app M).symm ≪≫
655	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (φ ∣_ (U i))).mapIso e ≪≫
656	    pullbackFreeIso (φ ∣_ (U i)) (ULift.{u} (Fin d))⟩
657	
658	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
659

281	namespace Scheme.Modules
282	
283	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
284	
285	/-- The **annihilator ideal sheaf** of a sheaf of modules `F` on a scheme `X`
286	(`def:modules_annihilator`).
287	
288	On each affine open `U`, the intended section is the annihilator
289	`Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` of the `Γ(X,U)`-module of sections `Γ(F,U)`. The ideal sheaf
290	is assembled with `Scheme.IdealSheafData.ofIdeals`, the largest ideal sheaf contained
291	in that affine-local family — exactly the construction used for `Scheme.Hom.ker`. This
292	sidesteps proving the basic-open coherence (`map_ideal_basicOpen`) at definition time;
293	the identity `(annihilator F).ideal U = Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` is the downstream
294	characterization lemma (`annihilator_ideal`, blocked on the QCoh localization bridge).
295	
296	This is a project-local primitive: Mathlib does not carry an annihilator ideal sheaf
297	for sheaves of modules on a scheme. -/
298	noncomputable def annihilator (F : X.Modules) : X.IdealSheafData :=
299	  IdealSheafData.ofIdeals fun U => Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1)
300	
301	/-- The component of the annihilator ideal sheaf at an affine open is contained in the
302	module annihilator of the sections. This is the always-available (`ofIdeals`) direction
303	of the characterization; the reverse inclusion is the basic-open coherence blocked on
304	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`. Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
305	lemma annihilator_ideal_le (F : X.Modules) (U : X.affineOpens) :
306	    (annihilator F).ideal U ≤ Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1) :=
307	  IdealSheafData.ideal_ofIdeals_le _ _
308	
309	/-- The **schematic support** of a sheaf of modules `F` on a scheme `X`
310	(`def:schematic_support`): the closed subscheme of `X` cut out by the annihilator
311	ideal sheaf `annihilator F`. Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
312	noncomputable def schematicSupport (F : X.Modules) : Scheme.{u} :=
313	  (annihilator F).subscheme
314	
315	/-- The canonical closed immersion of the schematic support into the ambient scheme,
316	realizing `schematicSupport F` as a closed subscheme of `X` (`def:schematic_support`).
317	This is the `IdealSheafData.subschemeι` of the annihilator ideal sheaf; it is a
318	`IsPreimmersion` + `QuasiCompact` immersion onto the support. Project-local because
319	`annihilator` is. -/
320	noncomputable def schematicSupportι (F : X.Modules) : schematicSupport F ⟶ X :=
321	  (annihilator F).subschemeι
322	
323	/-- The sheaf of modules `F` **has proper support over `S` along `f`**
324	(`def:has_proper_support`): the composite of the schematic-support immersion with
325	`f : X ⟶ S` is a proper morphism. Since `AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper` is stable under
326	base change, this condition is preserved by pullback, as required by the Quot functor's
327	pullback action. Project-local because `schematicSupport` is. -/
328	def HasProperSupport {S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
329	  IsProper (schematicSupportι F ≫ f)
330	
331	end Scheme.Modules
332	
333	end AlgebraicGeometry
334	
335	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — annihilator under localization
336	
337	The annihilator ideal sheaf `def:modules_annihilator` of a coherent sheaf is
338	built from the affine-local data `U ↦ Ann_{O(U)}(F(U))`, packaged as a
339	`Scheme.IdealSheafData`. The structure's coherence field `map_ideal_basicOpen`
340	requires the algebraic fact that, for a *finitely generated* module, the
341	annihilator commutes with localization:
342	`Ann(S⁻¹M) = (Ann M)·S⁻¹R`. Mathlib (at the pinned commit) does not carry this
343	lemma, so it is supplied here as the load-bearing engine for that construction.
344	-/
345	
346	namespace Module
347	
348	/-- For a finitely generated module `M` over a commutative ring `R`, the
349	annihilator commutes with localization: if `Rₚ` localizes `R` at a submonoid
350	`S` and `f : M →ₗ[R] Mₚ` localizes `M` at `S`, then the annihilator of `Mₚ`
351	over `Rₚ` is the extension (`Ideal.map` along `algebraMap R Rₚ`) of the
352	annihilator of `M` over `R`.
353	
354	This is the abstract `IsLocalization`/`IsLocalizedModule` form, matching the
355	shape needed for the affine-basic-open coherence of the annihilator ideal sheaf
356	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator`, `def:modules_annihilator`):
357	the structure-sheaf restriction `Γ(X,U) → Γ(X, D(f))` is
358	`IsLocalization (powers f)`, and for a quasi-coherent `F` the section
359	restriction is `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`.
360	
361	Mathlib has no annihilator-localization lemma, so this is project-local. -/
362	theorem annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map
363	    {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (S : Submonoid R)
364	    {Rₚ : Type*} [CommRing Rₚ] [Algebra R Rₚ] [IsLocalization S Rₚ]
365	    {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M]
366	    {Mₚ : Type*} [AddCommGroup Mₚ] [Module R Mₚ] [Module Rₚ Mₚ] [IsScalarTower R Rₚ Mₚ]
367	    (f : M →ₗ[R] Mₚ) [IsLocalizedModule S f] :
368	    Module.annihilator Rₚ Mₚ = (Module.annihilator R M).map (algebraMap R Rₚ) := by
369	  classical
370	  obtain ⟨t, htop⟩ := (Module.Finite.fg_top (R := R) (M := M))
371	  -- annihilating a spanning finset suffices for membership in the annihilator
372	  have key : ∀ (r : R), (∀ m ∈ t, r • m = 0) → r ∈ Module.annihilator R M := by
373	    intro r h
374	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator]
375	    intro x
376	    have hx : x ∈ Submodule.span R (t : Set M) := htop ▸ Submodule.mem_top
377	    induction hx using Submodule.span_induction with
378	    | mem y hy => exact h y hy
379	    | zero => simp
380	    | add a b _ _ ha hb => rw [smul_add, ha, hb, add_zero]
381	    | smul c a _ ha => rw [smul_comm, ha, smul_zero]
382	  apply le_antisymm
383	  · -- `Ann Rₚ Mₚ ⊆ (Ann R M).map`: clear one common denominator over the generators
384	    intro y hy
385	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator] at hy
386	    obtain ⟨⟨a, s⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalization.mk'_surjective S y
387	    dsimp only at hy ⊢
388	    have hgen : ∀ m ∈ t, ∃ u : S, (u : R) • a • m = 0 := by
389	      intro m hm
390	      have hz := hy (IsLocalizedModule.mk' f m (1 : S))
391	      rw [IsLocalizedModule.mk'_smul_mk' Rₚ f, IsLocalizedModule.mk'_eq_zero,
392	        IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff S f] at hz
393	      obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hz
394	      exact ⟨u, hu⟩
395	    choose u hu using hgen
396	    obtain ⟨U, hU⟩ : ∃ U : S, ∀ m ∈ t, (U : R) • a • m = 0 := by
397	      refine ⟨∏ x ∈ t.attach, u x.1 x.2, ?_⟩
398	      intro m hm
399	      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ :=
400	        Finset.dvd_prod_of_mem (fun x : t => u x.1 x.2) (Finset.mem_attach t ⟨m, hm⟩)

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.RegroupHelper

/-!
# Flat base change for the pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf (`i = 0`)

This file establishes the `i = 0` (direct-image) case of flat base change: the
formation of the pushforward `f_* F` of a quasi-coherent sheaf commutes with flat
base change on the target.

Throughout we work with a (commutative, eventually cartesian) square of schemes
```
  X' --g'--> X
  |f'        |f
  v          v
  S' --g---> S
```
recorded by morphisms `f : X ⟶ S`, `g : S' ⟶ S`, `f' : X' ⟶ S'`, `g' : X' ⟶ X`
with `g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`, and `F : X.Modules` a sheaf of modules on `X`.

The three main declarations are:

* `AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardBaseChangeMap` — the canonical base-change map
  `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)`, built as the adjoint mate of the unit of the
  `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-adjunction.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` — for `f` affine and the
  square cartesian, the base-change map is an isomorphism (affine case: tensor
  associativity).
* `AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso` — for `g` flat and `f`
  quasi-compact quasi-separated, the base-change map is an isomorphism.

See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`.

Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §"Cohomology and base change, I",
Tag 02KH.
-/

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

open Scheme.Modules

open scoped TensorProduct

variable {S S' X X' : Scheme.{u}}
  (f : X ⟶ S) (g : S' ⟶ S) (f' : X' ⟶ S') (g' : X' ⟶ X)

/-- The canonical base-change map for the pushforward of a sheaf of modules.

Given a commutative square
```
  X' --g'--> X
  |f'        |f
  v          v
  S' --g---> S
```
(with `comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`) and a sheaf of modules `F` on `X`, this is the
canonical morphism `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)` of sheaves of modules on `S'`.

It is the image, under the `(g^*, g_*)`-adjunction transpose, of the composite
```
  f_* F --f_*(unit)--> f_* (g')_* (g')^* F
        = (g' ≫ f)_* (g')^* F = (f' ≫ g)_* (g')^* F = g_* f'_* (g')^* F,
```
where `unit` is the unit of the `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-adjunction and the middle
equalities are the pseudofunctoriality of pushforward together with the
commutativity of the square.

Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §"Cohomology and base change, I",
base-change diagram. -/
noncomputable def pushforwardBaseChangeMap (comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g) (F : X.Modules) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F) ⟶

Let me study the `over`/`overRestrictEquiv` machinery in QuotScheme.lean — the sections-level restriction bridge that `glueRestrictionIso` will need.

753	The gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `lem:over_restrict_iso` (`overRestrictIso`) rests on an
754	equivalence of *sheaf* categories
755	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`
756	induced by the equivalence of underlying sites
757	`Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`. Mathlib carries `Opens.overEquivalence` but leaves
758	the *continuity* of its two functors and the induced sheaf-category equivalence as an explicit TODO
759	(see `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`: "show that both functors of the equivalence
760	`overEquivalence U` are continuous and induce an equivalence between
761	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A` and `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology U) A`").
762	
763	This section fills that TODO. The two cover-lifting (`IsCocontinuous`) facts are the substance:
764	a sieve covers in the Grothendieck-topology-over-`U` exactly when its image under the slice
765	forgetful functor covers in the ambient space, and that condition matches the pointwise covering
766	condition on the open subspace `↥U` because `Subtype.val : ↥U → X` is an injective open embedding.
767	From the two cocontinuities, `Equivalence.isDenseSubsite_inverse_of_isCocontinuous` produces the
768	dense-subsite hypothesis and `Equivalence.sheafCongr` produces the sheaf equivalence.
769	
770	It is the foundational (purely topological / topos-theoretic) layer of the slice-to-geometric
771	transport; the remaining steps toward `overRestrictIso` (identifying the sliced structure sheaf
772	`O_X.over U` with the open subscheme's structure sheaf `U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf` under this
773	equivalence, then lifting to sheaves of modules via `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` and relating
774	to `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor U.ι`) are the geometric layer above it. -/
775	
776	section OverSiteSheafEquivalence
777	
778	open TopologicalSpace Topology
779	
780	variable {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X)
781	
782	/-- The functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is cocontinuous (cover-lifting) from the
783	`U`-slice of the ambient Grothendieck topology to the Grothendieck topology of the open
784	subspace `↥U`. Foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `overRestrictIso`;
785	fills the `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` TODO. Project-local. -/
786	instance overEquivalence_functor_isCocontinuous :
787	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsCocontinuous
788	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) where
789	  cover_lift := by
790	    intro Y S hS
791	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff]

/-- The pseudofunctor from `Schemeᵒᵖ` to the bicategory of adjunctions which sends
a scheme `X` to the category `X.Modules` of sheaves of modules over `X`.
(This contains both the covariant and the contravariant functorialities of
these categories.) -/
@[simps! obj_obj map_l map_r map_adj
  mapId_hom_τl mapId_hom_τr mapId_inv_τl mapId_inv_τr
  mapComp_hom_τl mapComp_hom_τr mapComp_inv_τl mapComp_inv_τr]
def pseudofunctor :
    Pseudofunctor (LocallyDiscrete Scheme.{u}ᵒᵖ) (Adj Cat) :=
  LocallyDiscrete.mkPseudofunctor
    (fun X ↦ Adj.mk (Cat.of X.unop.Modules))
    (fun f ↦ .mk (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f.unop).toCat)
    (fun _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackId _))
        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardId _).symm))
    (fun _ _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackComp _ _).symm)
        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardComp _ _)))

end Functorial

noncomputable section Restriction

variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g]

/-- Restriction of an `𝒪ₓ`-module along an open immersion.
This is isomorphic to the pullback functor (see `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`)
but has better defeqs. -/
def restrictFunctor : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
  letI α : X.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ Y.presheaf := { app U := (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
  SheafOfModules.pushforward (F := f.opensFunctor)
    ⟨Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)⟩

/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
abbrev restrict (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] : X.Modules :=
  (restrictFunctor f).obj M

@[simp] lemma restrict_obj (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] (U) :
    Γ(M.restrict f, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U) := rfl

@[simp] lemma restrict_map (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] {U V} (i : U ⟶ V) :
    (M.restrict f).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op := rfl

/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
def restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso : pushforward f ⋙ restrictFunctor f ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp.{u} f.opensFunctor (Opens.map f.base)
    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y) (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _) ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents
      (fun U ↦ eqToIso (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm) fun _ ↦ rfl) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext U x; exact
      congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x)) ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

/-- Restriction is right adjoint to pushforward. -/
def restrictAdjunction : restrictFunctor f ⊣ pushforward f := by
  refine pushforwardPushforwardAdj (by exact f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction) _ _ ?_ ?_
  · ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x)
  · ext U x
    have : (f.appIso U.unop).inv ≫ f.app _ ≫
      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U.unop).symm).op = 𝟙 _ := by
      rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_app_assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← X.presheaf.map_id]; rfl
    exact congr($this x)

instance : IsIso (restrictAdjunction f).counit :=
  inferInstanceAs (IsIso <| (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso f).hom)

instance : (restrictFunctor f).IsLeftAdjoint := (restrictAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).Full := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.full
instance : (pushforward f).Faithful := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.faithful

@[simp]
lemma restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app (M : Y.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((restrictAdjunction f).unit.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (f.image_preimage_le U)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictAdjunction_counit_app_app (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens) :
    ((restrictAdjunction f).counit.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm).op := rfl

/-- Restriction is naturally isomorphic to the inverse image. -/
def restrictFunctorIsoPullback : restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f :=
  (restrictAdjunction f).leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)

/-- Restriction along the identity is isomorphic to the identity. -/
def restrictFunctorId : restrictFunctor (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents (fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp))) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr
      (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorId_hom_app_app :
    (restrictFunctorId.hom.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show U = 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U by simp)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorId_inv_app_app :
    (restrictFunctorId.inv.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U = U by simp)).op := rfl

/-- Restriction along the composition is isomorphic to the composition of restrictions. -/
def restrictFunctorComp : restrictFunctor (f ≫ g) ≅ restrictFunctor g ⋙ restrictFunctor f :=
  have : (f.opensFunctor ⋙ g.opensFunctor).IsContinuous
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Z) :=
    Functor.isContinuous_comp _ _ _ (Opens.grothendieckTopology _) _
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp)) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
    (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _).symm

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorComp_hom_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorComp_inv_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl

/-- Restriction along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def restrictFunctorCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g] :
    restrictFunctor f ≅ restrictFunctor g :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp [hf])) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; subst hf; simp)

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl

/-- Restriction along open immersions commutes with taking stalks. -/
def restrictStalkNatIso (x : X) :
    restrictFunctor f ⋙ toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ x ≅
    toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ (f x) :=
  haveI := Functor.initial_of_adjunction (f.isOpenEmbedding.adjunctionNhds x)
  (toPresheaf _ ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringLeft (OpenNhds (f x))ᵒᵖ Y.Opensᵒᵖ Ab).obj
      (OpenNhds.inclusion (f x)).op).isoWhiskerLeft

input.description: Check pullback existence hypotheses for SheafOfModules

content:
@[expose] public section

universe v v₁ v₂ v₃ v₄ u₁ u₂ u₃ u₄ u

open CategoryTheory Functor

namespace SheafOfModules

variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
  {D' : Type u₃} [Category.{v₃} D'] {D'' : Type u₄} [Category.{v₄} D'']
  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {F : C ⥤ D}
  {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
  [Functor.IsContinuous F J K]
  (φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R)

section

variable [(pushforward.{v} φ).IsRightAdjoint]

/-- The pullback functor `SheafOfModules S ⥤ SheafOfModules R` induced by
a morphism of sheaves of rings `S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R`,
defined as the left adjoint functor to the pushforward, when it exists. -/
noncomputable def pullback : SheafOfModules.{v} S ⥤ SheafOfModules.{v} R :=
  (pushforward.{v} φ).leftAdjoint

/-- Given a continuous functor between sites `F`, and a morphism of sheaves of rings
`S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R`, this is the adjunction
between the corresponding pullback and pushforward functors on the categories
of sheaves of modules. -/
noncomputable def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback.{v} φ ⊣ pushforward.{v} φ :=
  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (pushforward φ)

instance : (pullback.{v} φ).IsLeftAdjoint :=
  (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ).isLeftAdjoint

end

section

variable [(PresheafOfModules.pushforward.{v} φ.hom).IsRightAdjoint]
  [HasWeakSheafify K AddCommGrpCat.{v}] [K.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v}]

namespace PullbackConstruction

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- Construction of a left adjoint to the functor `pushforward.{v} φ` by using the
pullback of presheaves of modules and the sheafification. -/
noncomputable def adjunction :
    (forget S ⋙ PresheafOfModules.pullback.{v} φ.hom ⋙
      PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R₀ := R.obj) (𝟙 R.obj)) ⊣ pushforward.{v} φ :=
  Adjunction.mkOfHomEquiv
    { homEquiv := fun F G ↦
        ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 R.obj)).homEquiv _ _).trans
            (((PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ.hom).homEquiv F.val G.val).trans
              ((fullyFaithfulForget S).homEquiv (Y := (pushforward φ).obj G)).symm)
      homEquiv_naturality_left_symm := by
        intros
        dsimp [Functor.FullyFaithful.homEquiv]
        -- these erw seem difficult to remove
        erw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm,
          Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm]
        dsimp [pushforward_obj_val]
        simp only [Functor.map_comp, Category.assoc]
      homEquiv_naturality_right := by
        tauto }

end PullbackConstruction

instance : (pushforward.{v} φ).IsRightAdjoint :=
  (PullbackConstruction.adjunction.{v} φ).isRightAdjoint

/-- The pullback functor on sheaves of modules can be described as a composition
of the forget functor to presheaves, the pullback on presheaves of modules, and
the sheafification functor. -/
noncomputable def pullbackIso :
    pullback.{v} φ ≅
      forget S ⋙ PresheafOfModules.pullback.{v} φ.hom ⋙
        PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R₀ := R.obj) (𝟙 R.obj) :=
  Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ)
    (PullbackConstruction.adjunction φ)

# archon-protected.yaml
# Declarations whose signatures must not be modified by any agent.
# The mathematician owns these; agents are read-only on them.
#
# NOTE (extract 2026-06-05): the parent project's protected declarations
# (Jacobian/AbelJacobi/Genus) are all outside this subproject's scope and
# were removed with their files. This file is user-owned — if you want the
# seven seed declarations' signatures frozen, add:
#
# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean:
#   - AlgebraicGeometry.affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso
#   - AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso
# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean:
#   - AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness
# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean:
#   - AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial
#   - AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor
#   - AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian
#   - AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable
253:def IsLocallyFreeOfRank {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) (d : ℕ) : Prop :=

240	
241	/-- **Locally free of rank `d`** for a sheaf of modules on a scheme.
242	
243	A sheaf of modules `M` on a scheme `X` is *locally free of rank `d`* when `X`
244	admits an open cover `{U i}` on each member of which the restriction
245	`M|_{U i}` (the pullback of `M` along the open immersion `(U i).ι`) is
246	isomorphic to the free module of rank `d`, `O_{U i}^{⊕ d}` (encoded as
247	`SheafOfModules.free (ULift (Fin d))` over the structure-ring sheaf of the
248	open subscheme `(U i).toScheme`).
249	
250	This predicate is project-local: Mathlib does not supply a rank-indexed local
251	freeness predicate for sheaves of modules on a scheme. Blueprint:
252	`def:is_locally_free_of_rank` (Nitsure §1, Exercise (2)). -/
253	def IsLocallyFreeOfRank {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) (d : ℕ) : Prop :=
254	  ∃ (ι : Type u) (U : ι → X.Opens), (⨆ i, U i = ⊤) ∧
255	    ∀ i, Nonempty ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (U i).ι).obj M ≅
256	      _root_.SheafOfModules.free (R := (U i).toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (ULift.{u} (Fin d)))
257	
258	end SheafOfModules
259	
260	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — annihilator ideal sheaf and schematic support
261	
262	These declarations build the annihilator ideal sheaf of a sheaf of modules and the
263	support/properness predicates of `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`,
264	§"Support and freeness predicates". Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no
265	annihilator ideal sheaf for sheaves of modules on a scheme, nor a schematic-support
266	or proper-support predicate, so they are built here.
267	
268	The annihilator is packaged via `Scheme.IdealSheafData.ofIdeals`, exactly mirroring
269	Mathlib's `Scheme.Hom.ker` (which is `ofIdeals fun U ↦ RingHom.ker (f.app U).hom`):
270	`ofIdeals` produces *the largest ideal sheaf contained in* an arbitrary affine-local
271	family of ideals, so the structure's `map_ideal_basicOpen` coherence is discharged
272	internally and need not be supplied at definition time. The basic-open coherence that
273	makes the local annihilators agree with `ofIdeals` (the analogue of `Hom.ker_apply`,
274	`def:modules_annihilator`) is the separate characterization lemma `annihilator_ideal`,
275	which depends on the not-yet-closed QCoh→localization bridge
276	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen` (`lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`) together with
277	the algebra engine `Module.annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map`
278	(`lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map`); see the handoff in
279	`task_results/.../QuotScheme.md`. -/
280	
281	namespace Scheme.Modules

1619	The overlap condition consumed by `glueLift` is an equation between composites of adjoint
1620	transposes along the chart immersions. The next two lemmas transpose each leg back across
1621	the pullback–pushforward adjunction of the *composite* overlap immersion, exposing the
1622	pullback-level identity `g_{ij} ∘ f_ij^* u^i = (t_ij ≫ f_ji)^* u^j` that the matrix
1623	computation closes. The first handles the unit/`pushforwardComp` factor pair, the second
1624	the trailing `pushforwardCongr` reindexing cast. -/
1625	
1626	/-- **Leg transpose** (`lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose` engine): for
1627	`b : V ⟶ U`, `a : U ⟶ X` and `c : a^* W ⟶ M`, the descent-equalizer leg
1628	`homEquiv_a(c) ≫ (a_* unit_b) ≫ pushforwardComp` is the transpose along the composite
1629	`b ≫ a` of the pullback of `c` to `V` (through the pseudofunctor comparison
1630	`pullbackComp`). Combines `homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app` with Mathlib's
1631	`conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv`. Project-local. -/
1632	lemma homEquiv_comp_unit_pushforwardComp {V U X : Scheme.{u}} (b : V ⟶ U) (a : U ⟶ X)
1633	    {W : X.Modules} {M : U.Modules} (c : (Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj W ⟶ M) :
1634	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).homEquiv W M c ≫
1635	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward a).map
1636	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app M) ≫
1637	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp b a).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj M))
1638	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (b ≫ a)).homEquiv W
1639	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj M)
1640	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).inv.app W ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map c) := by
1641	  -- inner transpose: `c ≫ unit_b` is the `b`-transpose of `(pullback b).map c`
1642	  have h2 : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).homEquiv
1643	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj W) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj M)
1644	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map c)
1645	      = c ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app M := by
1646	    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]
1647	    exact ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.naturality c).symm
1648	  -- composite-adjunction transpose factors through the two single transposes
1649	  have h3 : ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).comp
1650	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b)).homEquiv W
1651	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj M) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map c)
1652	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).homEquiv _ _
1653	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).homEquiv _ _
1654	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map c)) := by
1655	    rw [Adjunction.comp_homEquiv]
1656	    rfl
1657	  -- mate compatibility: precomposing with `pullbackComp.inv` is postcomposition by the
1658	  -- conjugate, which Mathlib identifies as `pushforwardComp.hom`
1659	  have h4 := homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app
1660	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).comp
1661	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b))
1662	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (b ≫ a))
1663	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).inv
1664	      (f := (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map c)
1665	  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv] at h4
1666	  rw [h4, h3, h2]
1667	  -- regroup and fold the unit factor back into the transpose (term-mode: a positional
1668	  -- `rw [homEquiv_naturality_right]` matches inside the wrong `homEquiv` argument)
1669	  exact (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans
1670	    (congrArg (· ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp b a).hom.app
1671	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj M))
1672	      (Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right _ _ _).symm)
1673	
1674	/-- **Congruence-cast transpose**: postcomposing a transpose along `e` with the
1675	`pushforwardCongr` cast of an equality `e = e'` is the transpose along `e'` of the
1676	`pullbackCongr`-reindexed morphism. Generic `subst` lemma. Project-local. -/
1677	lemma homEquiv_comp_pushforwardCongr {V X : Scheme.{u}} {e e' : V ⟶ X} (h : e = e')
1678	    {W : X.Modules} {N : V.Modules} (y : (Scheme.Modules.pullback e).obj W ⟶ N) :
1679	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction e).homEquiv W N y ≫
1680	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr h).hom.app N
1681	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction e').homEquiv W N
1682	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h).inv.app W ≫ y) := by
1683	  subst h
1684	  have h1 : (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr (rfl : e = e)).hom.app N = 𝟙 _ := by
1685	    ext U
1686	    simp
1687	  have h2 : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (rfl : e = e)).inv.app W = 𝟙 _ := by
1688	    simp [Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr]
1689	  rw [h1, h2, Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]
1690	
1691	/-- **Transposed form of the `glueLift` overlap condition**: the `(i,j)`-component of the
1692	equalizing hypothesis consumed by `glueLift` holds iff the pullback-level identity
1693	`f_ij^* (c i) = congr ∘ (t_ij ≫ f_ji)^* (c j) ∘ g_ij⁻¹` does (all comparisons through the
1694	pseudofunctor casts). Both legs are transposed along the composite overlap immersion via
1695	`homEquiv_comp_unit_pushforwardComp` / `homEquiv_comp_pushforwardCongr`, and the
1696	hom-equivalence is injective. Project-local. -/
1697	lemma glueLift_cond_iff (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u})
1698	    (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
1699	    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
1700	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))

@[expose] public section

universe v v₁ v₂ v₃ v₄ u₁ u₂ u₃ u₄ u

open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite Functor

namespace PresheafOfModules

section

variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
  {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
  [(pushforward.{v} φ).IsRightAdjoint]

/-- The pullback functor `PresheafOfModules S ⥤ PresheafOfModules R` induced by
a morphism of presheaves of rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, defined as the left adjoint
functor to the pushforward, when it exists. -/
noncomputable def pullback : PresheafOfModules.{v} S ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} R :=
  (pushforward.{v} φ).leftAdjoint

/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, this is the adjunction
between associated pullback and pushforward functors on the categories
of presheaves of modules. -/
noncomputable def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback.{v} φ ⊣ pushforward.{v} φ :=
  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (pushforward φ)

/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, this is the property
that the (partial) left adjoint functor of `pushforward φ` is defined
on a certain object `M : PresheafOfModules S`. -/
abbrev pullbackObjIsDefined : ObjectProperty (PresheafOfModules.{v} S) :=
  (pushforward φ).leftAdjointObjIsDefined

end

section

variable {C D : Type u} [SmallCategory C] [SmallCategory D]
  {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)

/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, where `F : C ⥤ D`,
`S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat`, `R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat` and `X : C`, the (partial) left adjoint
functor of `pushforward φ` is defined on the object `(free S).obj (yoneda.obj X)`:
this object shall be mapped to `(free R).obj (yoneda.obj (F.obj X))`. -/
noncomputable def pushforwardCompCoyonedaFreeYonedaCorepresentableBy (X : C) :
    (pushforward φ ⋙ coyoneda.obj (op ((free S).obj (yoneda.obj X)))).CorepresentableBy
      ((free R).obj (yoneda.obj (F.obj X))) where
  homEquiv {M} := freeYonedaEquiv.trans
    (freeYonedaEquiv (M := (pushforward φ).obj M)).symm
  homEquiv_comp {M N} g f := freeYonedaEquiv.injective (by
    dsimp
    erw [Equiv.apply_symm_apply, freeYonedaEquiv_comp]
    conv_rhs => erw [freeYonedaEquiv_comp]
    erw [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
    rfl)

lemma pullbackObjIsDefined_free_yoneda (X : C) :
    pullbackObjIsDefined φ ((free S).obj (yoneda.obj X)) :=
  (pushforwardCompCoyonedaFreeYonedaCorepresentableBy φ X).isCorepresentable

lemma pullbackObjIsDefined_eq_top :
    pullbackObjIsDefined.{u} φ = ⊤ := by
  ext M
  simp only [Pi.top_apply, Prop.top_eq_true, iff_true]
  apply leftAdjointObjIsDefined_of_isColimit
    M.isColimitFreeYonedaCoproductsCokernelCofork
  rintro (_ | _)
  all_goals
    apply leftAdjointObjIsDefined_colimit _
      (fun _ ↦ pullbackObjIsDefined_free_yoneda _ _)

instance : (pushforward.{u} φ).IsRightAdjoint :=
  isRightAdjoint_of_leftAdjointObjIsDefined_eq_top
    (pullbackObjIsDefined_eq_top φ)

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

/-- **Unit coherence (`map_id` keystone, `lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id`).** The
Mathlib free-pullback comparison `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit` at the identity
morphism agrees, on the unit sheaf, with the scheme-level pseudofunctor identity
`Scheme.Modules.pullbackId`. Project-local: bridges `pullbackObjFreeIso` to the
pseudofunctor `pullbackId`. -/
lemma pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id {T : Scheme.{u}} :
    SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (𝟙 T))
      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId T).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf) := by
  rw [← SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_symm_unitToPushforwardObjUnit,
    Equiv.symm_apply_eq, Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]
  have h := CategoryTheory.unit_conjugateEquiv Adjunction.id

import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GrassmannianCells
import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme

/-!
# The tautological quotient and the universal property of `Gr(r,d)`

This file adds, on top of the Grassmannian scheme `Gr(d,r)` built in
`GrassmannianCells.lean`, the tautological rank-`d` quotient
`u : O^r ↠ U` and the universal property making `Gr(d,r)` the fine moduli
space of rank-`d` locally free quotients of `O^r`.

Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianQuot.tex` (Nitsure §1).
-/

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — scalar endomorphisms of the structure sheaf

To realise a matrix of regular functions as a morphism of free sheaves of modules we
need to turn a global section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)` of the structure sheaf into a scalar
endomorphism of `O_X` (= `SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf`). On the affine chart
`U^I = Spec R^I` the matrix entries of the universal matrix `X^I` live in `R^I`, and we
inject them into the global sections via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`.

These helpers are project-local: Mathlib has no ready-made "matrix ↦ morphism of free
sheaves" primitive. -/
Now writing batch 1. Quick API name checks first:

/-! ### Restriction of the glued sheaf to a chart (`def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso`)

The glued sheaf is the descent equalizer `eq(a, b) ⊆ ∏ᵢ (ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ`. This section
re-exposes the two legs of that equalizer as standalone declarations
(`glueLegA`/`glueLegB`), records that `glue` *is* their equalizer (`glueIsoEqualizer`,
definitional), and produces the canonical projection `glueProj i` of the glued sheaf
onto the `i`-th pushforward factor together with its compatibility with `glueLift`.
The adjoint transpose of `glueProj i` along the chart immersion `D.ι i` is the
*restriction morphism* `glueRestrictionHom i : ιᵢ^* (glue …) ⟶ M i`; effective descent
(consuming the cocycle hypotheses C1/C2) makes it an isomorphism — the restriction
isomorphism `glueRestrictionIso` of `def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso`. The
limit-preservation engine is `restrictFunctor`: pullback along an open immersion is
naturally isomorphic to a site-level pushforward, which is a right adjoint, hence
preserves the descent equalizer and the pushforward product. -/

section GlueRestriction

variable (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)

/-- The product of pushforwards `∏ᵢ (ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ` into which the glued sheaf embeds.
Project-local helper re-exposing the `P` of `glue`. -/
noncomputable def glueProd : D.glued.Modules :=
  ∏ᶜ fun i => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i)

/-- The overlap product `∏_{(i,j)} (f_ij ≫ ιᵢ)_* (f_ij^* Mᵢ)` receiving the two descent
legs. Project-local helper re-exposing the `Qfun`-product of `glue`. -/
noncomputable def glueOverlapProd : D.glued.Modules :=
  ∏ᶜ fun p : D.J × D.J =>
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1)).obj
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))

/-- First descent leg (`a` of `glue`): on the `(i,j)`-component, restrict the `i`-th
chart section to the overlap `V (i,j)` via the unit of the pullback–pushforward
adjunction along `f_ij` and the pushforward-composition comparison. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def glueLegA : glueProd D M ⟶ glueOverlapProd D M :=
  Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.1 ≫
    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app (M p.1)) ≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p.1 p.2) (D.ι p.1)).hom.app
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))

/-- Second descent leg (`b` of `glue`): on the `(i,j)`-component, restrict the `j`-th
chart section, transport it across the transition isomorphism `g_ij`, and reindex the
immersion via the glue condition. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def glueLegB
    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j)) :
    glueProd D M ⟶ glueOverlapProd D M :=
  Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.2 ≫
    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
          (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) (D.ι p.2)).hom.app
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).obj (M p.2)) ≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
        ((D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2)).map (g p.1 p.2).inv ≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
        (show (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2 = D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1 by
          rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p.1 p.2)).hom.app
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))

variable (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
  (hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
      (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
  (hC2 : ∀ i j k,
      pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
          (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (g i j) ≪≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_mid D i j k)).app (M j) ≪≫
        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i))
          (D.f j k) (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j) (g j k) ≪≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_tgt D i j k)).app (M k)
      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_src D i j k)).app (M i) ≪≫
        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
          (D.f i k) (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) (g i k))

/-- The glued sheaf *is* the equalizer of the two (re-exposed) descent legs. The
isomorphism is definitional (`Iso.refl`); it exists to let the equalizer API fire
syntactically on `glue`. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def glueIsoEqualizer :
    glue D M g hC1 hC2 ≅ equalizer (glueLegA D M) (glueLegB D M g) :=
  Iso.refl _

/-- Projection of the glued sheaf onto the `i`-th pushforward factor `(ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ`:
the equalizer inclusion followed by the product projection. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def glueProj (i : D.J) :
    glue D M g hC1 hC2 ⟶ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i) :=
  (glueIsoEqualizer D M g hC1 hC2).hom ≫ equalizer.ι (glueLegA D M) (glueLegB D M g) ≫
    Pi.π _ i

/-- `glueLift` followed by the `i`-th projection recovers the `i`-th component of the
lifted family. Project-local. -/
@[reassoc]
lemma glueLift_glueProj {W : D.glued.Modules}
    (k : ∀ i, W ⟶ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i))
    (hk : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
      k p.1 ≫
          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app (M p.1)) ≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p.1 p.2) (D.ι p.1)).hom.app
            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))
        = k p.2 ≫
          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
              (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) (D.ι p.2)).hom.app
            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).obj (M p.2)) ≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
            ((D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2)).map (g p.1 p.2).inv ≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
            (show (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2 = D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1 by
              rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p.1 p.2)).hom.app
            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))) (i : D.J) :
    glueLift D M g hC1 hC2 k hk ≫ glueProj D M g hC1 hC2 i = k i := by
  dsimp only [glueLift, glueProj, glueIsoEqualizer, Iso.refl_hom]
  rw [Category.id_comp, ← Category.assoc, equalizer.lift_ι, Limits.Pi.lift_π]

/-- **The restriction morphism of the glued sheaf** to the `i`-th chart: the adjoint
transpose, along the chart immersion `ιᵢ`, of the `i`-th projection `glueProj i`.
Effective descent (`isIso_glueRestrictionHom`) makes it an isomorphism. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def glueRestrictionHom (i : D.J) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj (glue D M g hC1 hC2) ⟶ M i :=
  ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.ι i)).homEquiv _ _).symm
    (glueProj D M g hC1 hC2 i)

end GlueRestriction

/-- `restrictFunctor f` along an open immersion is a right adjoint: it is a site-level
pushforward of sheaves of modules, whose left adjoint (the site-level pullback) exists
by the presheaf-pullback + sheafification construction. Project-local instance. -/
instance restrictFunctor_isRightAdjoint {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] :
    (restrictFunctor f).IsRightAdjoint := by
  delta restrictFunctor
  infer_instance

/-- `restrictFunctor f` along an open immersion preserves limits (it is a right
adjoint). Project-local. -/
noncomputable instance restrictFunctor_preservesLimits.{w, w'} {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
    (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] :
    PreservesLimitsOfSize.{w, w'} (restrictFunctor f) :=
  (Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (restrictFunctor f)).rightAdjoint_preservesLimits

/-- **Pullback of sheaves of modules along an open immersion preserves limits**: it is
naturally isomorphic to `restrictFunctor f`, a site-level pushforward and right
adjoint. This is the engine that lets the chart restriction commute with the descent
equalizer. Project-local. -/
instance pullback_preservesLimits_of_isOpenImmersion.{w, w'} {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
    (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] :
    PreservesLimitsOfSize.{w, w'} (Scheme.Modules.pullback f) :=
  preservesLimits_of_natIso (restrictFunctorIsoPullback f)

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

/-- An equivalence relation on `Σ i, D.U i` that holds iff `𝖣.ι i x = 𝖣.ι j y`.
See `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData.ι_eq_iff`. -/
def Rel (a b : Σ i, ((D.U i).carrier : Type _)) : Prop :=
  ∃ x : (D.V (a.1, b.1)).carrier, D.f _ _ x = a.2 ∧ (D.t _ _ ≫ D.f _ _) x = b.2

theorem ι_eq_iff (i j : D.J) (x : (D.U i).carrier) (y : (D.U j).carrier) :
    𝖣.ι i x = 𝖣.ι j y ↔ D.Rel ⟨i, x⟩ ⟨j, y⟩ := by
  refine Iff.trans ?_
    (TopCat.GlueData.ι_eq_iff_rel
      D.toLocallyRingedSpaceGlueData.toSheafedSpaceGlueData.toPresheafedSpaceGlueData.toTopGlueData
      i j x y)
  rw [← ((TopCat.mono_iff_injective D.isoCarrier.inv).mp _).eq_iff, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
  · simp_rw [← D.ι_isoCarrier_inv]
    rfl -- `rfl` was not needed before https://github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4/pull/13170
  · infer_instance

theorem isOpen_iff (U : Set D.glued.carrier) : IsOpen U ↔ ∀ i, IsOpen (D.ι i ⁻¹' U) := by
  rw [← (TopCat.homeoOfIso D.isoCarrier.symm).isOpen_preimage, TopCat.GlueData.isOpen_iff]
  apply forall_congr'
  intro i
  rw [← Set.preimage_comp, ← ι_isoCarrier_inv]
  rfl

/-- The open cover of the glued space given by the glue data. -/

instance pullback_preservesLimits_of_isOpenImmersion.{w, w'} {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
    (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] :
    PreservesLimitsOfSize.{w, w'} (Scheme.Modules.pullback f) :=
  preservesLimits_of_natIso (restrictFunctorIsoPullback f)

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
input.new_string:
instance pullback_preservesLimits_of_isOpenImmersion.{w, w'} {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
    (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] :
    PreservesLimitsOfSize.{w, w'} (Scheme.Modules.pullback f) :=
  preservesLimits_of_natIso (restrictFunctorIsoPullback f)

/-- **Overlap-square opens identity**: for an open `V` of the `i`-th chart of a scheme
glue datum, the preimage under `ι_j` of its image in the glued scheme coincides with
the image, under the `j`-side overlap immersion `t_ij ≫ f_ji`, of its preimage under
`f_ij`. This is the underlying-opens form of the cartesianness of the chart-overlap
square (`vPullbackCone`); it is the site-level input identifying the two composite
restriction functors of the overlap square. Project-local. -/
lemma glueData_preimage_image_eq (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (i j : D.J)
    (V : (D.U i).Opens) :
    (D.ι j) ⁻¹ᵁ ((D.ι i) ''ᵁ V) = (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ''ᵁ ((D.f i j) ⁻¹ᵁ V) := by
  ext x
  constructor
  · intro hx
    -- a point of `U_j` mapping into `ι_i(V)` comes from the overlap via the glue relation
    obtain ⟨y, hyV, hyx⟩ := hx
    obtain ⟨w, hw1, hw2⟩ := (D.ι_eq_iff i j y x).mp hyx
    exact ⟨w, show w ∈ (D.f i j) ⁻¹ᵁ V from hw1 ▸ hyV, hw2⟩
  · rintro ⟨w, hwV, rfl⟩
    refine ⟨D.f i j w, hwV, ?_⟩
    -- `ι_i (f_ij w) = ι_j ((t_ij ≫ f_ji) w)`: the glue condition at the point `w`
    have h := congrArg (fun m : D.V (i, j) ⟶ D.glued => m.base w)
      ((D.glue_condition i j).symm.trans (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm)
    simpa using h
  sorry

/-- **Effective descent: the chart restriction morphism of the glued sheaf is an
isomorphism** (`def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso`). This is where the cocycle
hypotheses (C1)/(C2) are consumed.

PROOF ROUTE (scoped iter-066, partially built): the chart pullback `ι_i^*` preserves
limits (`pullback_preservesLimits_of_isOpenImmersion` — it is isomorphic to the
site-level pushforward `restrictFunctor`), so `ι_i^* (glue …)` is the equalizer of the
restricted legs `ι_i^* (glueLegA)`, `ι_i^* (glueLegB)` and the restricted product
embeds into `∏_j ι_i^* ((ι_j)_* M_j)`. The candidate inverse `M i ⟶ ι_i^* (glue …)`
is the equalizer lift of the family whose `j`-component transports a section of `M i`
to the overlap: `unit_{f_ij} ≫ (f_ij)_* (g_ij-conjugate) ≫ β_ij⁻¹`, where
`β_ij : ι_i^* ((ι_j)_* M_j) ≅ (f_ij)_* ((t_ij ≫ f_ji)^* M_j)` is the open-cover base
change of the cartesian overlap square (site-level: both composites are pushforwards
along the SAME opens functor, by `glueData_preimage_image_eq`). The equalizing
condition of that family is (C2) in transported form; the two triangle identities
reduce to (C1) and the counit triangle. Remaining work: construct `β_ij` (via
`restrictFunctor` + `SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp`/`pushforwardCongr` +
`glueData_preimage_image_eq`) and verify the three conditions. -/
theorem isIso_glueRestrictionHom (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
    (hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
        (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
    (hC2 : ∀ i j k,
        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
            (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (g i j) ≪≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_mid D i j k)).app (M j) ≪≫
          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i))
            (D.f j k) (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j) (g j k) ≪≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_tgt D i j k)).app (M k)
        = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_src D i j k)).app (M i) ≪≫
          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
            (D.f i k) (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) (g i k)) (i : D.J) :
    IsIso (glueRestrictionHom D M g hC1 hC2 i) := by
  sorry

/-- **The restriction isomorphism of the glued sheaf**
(`def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso`): the canonical identification
`ι_i^* (glue D M g …) ≅ M i` of the chart restriction of the glued sheaf with the
`i`-th input sheaf, with underlying morphism the adjoint transpose of the `i`-th
descent-equalizer projection. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def glueRestrictionIso (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
    (hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
        (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
    (hC2 : ∀ i j k,
        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
            (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (g i j) ≪≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_mid D i j k)).app (M j) ≪≫
          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i))
            (D.f j k) (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j) (g j k) ≪≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_tgt D i j k)).app (M k)
        = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_src D i j k)).app (M i) ≪≫
          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
            (D.f i k) (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) (g i k)) (i : D.J) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj (glue D M g hC1 hC2) ≅ M i :=
  haveI := isIso_glueRestrictionHom D M g hC1 hC2 i
  asIso (glueRestrictionHom D M g hC1 hC2 i)

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

/-- **`Gr(d,r)` represents the Grassmannian functor** (`thm:grassmannian_universal_property`):
the tautological quotient `⟨U, u⟩` exhibits `Gr(d,r)` as the fine moduli space of rank-`d`
quotients of `O^r`, i.e. `Hom(T, Gr(d,r)) ≅ Grass(r,d)(T)` naturally in `T`.

NOTE: `functor`, `Scheme.Modules.glue`, `universalQuotient` and `tautologicalQuotient` have
all landed (axiom-clean); no upstream gap remains. Body = the local-to-global inverse
construction of Nitsure §1 (a `T`-point of `Gr` is a rank-`d` quotient — this direction needs

universal property of the glued scheme). -/
